hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — 7, J. Both players called a raise before the cards were dealt.
Turn — 6. Your opponent led out with a bet. You called.
River — Jack. Your opponent fired again — half the pot.

wrongReasons: K 9 plays similarly, but the correct hand pairs a specific board card that K 9 doesn't. Look at which hole card ranks actually appear on the board.
With only two pair, 7 6 wouldn't commit chips across multiple streets. This betting line requires a genuinely made hand from the flop onward.
With only high card, 4 3 wouldn't commit chips across multiple streets. This betting line requires a genuinely made hand from the flop onward.
hand.explanation:
Answer: 6 9

Your opponent flopped a straight and immediately raised to build the pot. They led every remaining street — consistent value betting across all streets is a strong signal of a made hand.
